executiveSummary.text: Risk regime remains constructive but more fragile: S&P 500 (-0.38%) and Nasdaq (-0.13%) pulled back after intraday records while VIX stayed low at 17.08. Base case 55%: AI-led melt-up continues with higher volatility; 30%: oil/rates shock compresses long-duration multiples; 15%: broader risk-off. Confidence: medium.

Oil is the key macro transmission today: WTI rebounded to about $97.04 (+2.35% on Yahoo ticker strip) after CNBC reported WTI settled at $94.81 and Brent at $100.06 as Iran-US deal expectations remained unresolved. 1st order: oil lifts headline inflation; 2nd order: fewer Fed cuts / higher real yields; 3rd order: pressure on QQQ, semis, REITs and possibly gold despite geopolitical stress.

AI/semiconductor narrative is still bid but crowded: CNBC said the pullback included Amazon, Broadcom and Micron while fresh articles highlight AI-driven chip demand. Base case 60%: earnings/capex support persists; 25%: valuation air pocket if yields rise; 15%: thesis damage only if hyperscaler capex guidance rolls over. Confidence: medium.

Singapore remains defensive but not immune: STI closed +0.30% at 4,941.96; UOB reported Q1 net profit down 4% YoY to about S$1.44b but above expectations. 1st order: rate headwinds pressure NIM; 2nd order: wealth/fees cushion banks; 3rd order: bank dividends remain sturdier than REIT rerating if global yields stay elevated.

Gold around $4,705 is a real-rate trade, not a crisis trade. With 10Y nominal at 4.41% and 10Y TIPS real yield at 1.96% (US Treasury May 7), gold upside requires falling real yields or stronger debasement demand; an oil-driven inflation/Fed-hold shock can hurt gold versus cash. Confidence: medium-high.

usGlobal.what: US equities pulled back after record intraday levels; CNBC reported S&P 500 -0.38%, Nasdaq -0.13%, Dow -313.62 points, with losses in Amazon, Broadcom and Micron.
usGlobal.why: This is valuation/regime news rather than thesis damage. 1st order: overbought AI leaders pause; 2nd order: rising oil/yields can lift discount rates; 3rd order: cash optionality becomes more valuable if semis sell off without capex deterioration.
usGlobal.implication: Do not chase strength blindly. Add only on pre-defined drawdowns unless AI capex/earnings breadth improves further. Probability: 55% melt-up continues, 30% multiple compression, 15% broader risk-off.

usGlobal.what: Fresh semiconductor coverage highlighted AI-fueled demand lifting chip sales, while CNA/Bloomberg-style commentary warned semis have carried a large share of S&P 500 returns.
usGlobal.why: 1st order: demand supports NVDA/TSM/semicap earnings; 2nd order: concentration increases index fragility; 3rd order: if AI demand stays strong but valuations reset, equipment and foundry names may offer better risk-adjusted adds than the most crowded leaders.
usGlobal.implication: Stay overweight scarcity assets, but separate thesis strength from entry price. Favor staged buys on weakness.

usGlobal.what: Markets are focused on the April jobs report after Yahoo Finance reported investors are bracing for the release.
usGlobal.why: 1st order: stronger payrolls/wages push yields up; 2nd order: Fed cut odds fall and real yields rise; 3rd order: QQQ/semis/REITs face multiple pressure while cash yield remains competitive. Weak labor does the opposite but could raise recession risk.
usGlobal.implication: Treat jobs as the next regime checkpoint, not a single-stock catalyst.

singapore.what: STI closed +0.30% at 4,941.96, in line with regional gains; Business Times/Straits Times reported Singapore stocks rose on May 7.
singapore.why: 1st order: regional risk appetite supports STI; 2nd order: banks dominate index resilience; 3rd order: if US yields stay high, bank income defensiveness may outperform REIT duration even inside the same Singapore dividend bucket.
singapore.implication: Hold SG dividend sleeve. Rebalance only if STI strength creates overconcentration versus FI liquidity target.

singapore.what: UOB Q1 net profit fell 4% YoY to roughly S$1.44b but beat consensus; wealth income rose and management kept a resilient-growth framing.
singapore.why: 1st order: lower rates/softer operating environment pressure NIM; 2nd order: wealth and fee income diversify earnings; 3rd order: banks remain better positioned than rate-sensitive REITs if real yields stay elevated.
singapore.implication: No thesis break. Watch whether NIM pressure spreads to DBS/OCBC guidance; dividends remain core unless credit costs rise.

commodities.what: Oil remained volatile around Iran-US negotiations; CNBC reported WTI settled at $94.81 and Brent at $100.06, while Yahoo's ticker strip later showed WTI near $97.04.
commodities.why: 1st order: oil shock feeds inflation; 2nd order: Fed cannot ease as quickly and real yields can rise; 3rd order: long-duration AI/REIT multiples and gold can fall even as geopolitical headlines worsen.
commodities.implication: Oil path is the highest-signal macro variable today. Crisis headline alone is not bullish gold; real-yield path decides.

commodities.what: Gold hovered around $4,705 while current Treasury data showed 10Y real yield at 1.96%.
commodities.why: 1st order: gold competes against real cash/bonds; 2nd order: if oil lifts inflation and Fed holds, real rates may stay high; 3rd order: gold can underperform cash despite geopolitical stress unless debasement demand overwhelms yield drag.
commodities.implication: Hold metals as structural hedge, but do not add on geopolitical headlines without a real-yield trigger.

narratives.title: AI scarcity thesis intact, but crowding risk rising
narratives.description: Fresh AI/semiconductor coverage supports the demand side, but CNBC's market recap showed semis among the pullback contributors. Transmission: AI capex supports earnings (1st), crowded ownership amplifies drawdowns when yields rise (2nd), and forced de-risking can create better entries in semicap/equipment names without thesis damage (3rd).
narratives.source: CNBC live market updates; Yahoo/Brave semiconductor search results within 24h

narratives.title: Oil-rates-real-yields is the dominant macro chain
narratives.description: Iran-US negotiation headlines are moving oil. The correct chain is oil -> inflation impulse -> Fed cut odds/real yields -> duration assets and gold. This is not a simple geopolitical safe-haven story.
narratives.source: CNBC live market updates; US Treasury May 7 yield/real-yield data
narratives.priceImpact: Negative for duration if oil stays high; mixed for banks; conditional for gold

narratives.title: Singapore banks remain income anchors, not high-beta growth
narratives.description: UOB's Q1 beat despite lower YoY profit reinforces the role of SG banks as dividend anchors with fee/wealth optionality. The constraint is NIM/credit-cost pressure if the cycle weakens.
narratives.source: Reuters/CNA/Business Times UOB reports within 24h; Yahoo STI quote
narratives.priceImpact: Neutral-to-positive for income; capped rerating if rates fall too quickly

secondOrderEffects.text: Oil up is not automatically good for metals. Oil -> inflation -> Fed delay -> higher real yields can pressure gold and REITs even if headlines feel risk-off.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Use real yields, not geopolitical emotion, as the metals add/trim signal.

secondOrderEffects.text: Strong jobs would look bullish for earnings at first, but if it lifts yields, the 2nd-order effect can be negative for QQQ/semis via discount-rate compression.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Post-jobs price action should be read through yields first, not headline payrolls alone.

secondOrderEffects.text: AI capex strength supports semis directly, but concentration means index risk rises: if a few AI leaders stumble, passive VWRA/QQQ drawdowns can exceed single-name thesis damage.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Keep cash deployment staged; do not let broad index exposure obscure AI concentration.

secondOrderEffects.text: Lower rates help REIT valuations first order, but if lower rates come from growth stress, tenant demand and financing access can deteriorate second order.
secondOrderEffects.implication: For Ascendas/CICT/MLT/FCT, prefer evidence of both lower yields and stable operating metrics.

investment.thesis: Staged accumulation of AI infrastructure scarcity names on valuation pullbacks, not headline momentum.
investment.trigger: 10-15% drawdown in semicap/foundry basket while hyperscaler capex guidance and AI order books remain intact; preferably with 10Y real yield stable/falling below ~1.85%.
investment.whyNow: AI demand remains strong, but market is crowded and oil/rates can cause non-thesis drawdowns.
investment.invalidator: Hyperscaler capex cuts, HBM/foundry order cancellations, or semicap book-to-bill deterioration.

investment.thesis: Maintain SG bank dividend core as FI stabilizer while using REIT exposure selectively.
investment.trigger: Bank selloff driven by NIM fears without credit-cost deterioration; for REITs, wait for lower real yields plus stable occupancy/renewal spreads.
investment.whyNow: UOB results show rate pressure but resilient diversified income; elevated real yields still cap REIT rerating.
investment.invalidator: Material credit-cost spike, dividend-policy change, or Singapore growth slowdown that hits both loans and asset quality.

trading.thesis: Do not chase QQQ/semis ahead of jobs/oil resolution; use cash as optionality.
trading.trigger: If jobs are hot and 10Y real yield rises above ~2.05%, wait for multiple compression before adding; if jobs are soft but earnings breadth holds, deploy a small tranche.
trading.whyNow: Next 24-48h macro data can dominate near-term price action even if AI fundamentals remain strong.
trading.invalidator: Yields fall and semis break to new highs on broad earnings revisions, reducing pullback odds.

trading.thesis: Gold add only on real-yield confirmation, not crisis headlines.
trading.trigger: 10Y TIPS real yield falls below ~1.85% or gold holds strength while real yields rise, indicating debasement demand is overpowering yield drag.
trading.whyNow: Oil/geopolitical headlines are noisy; gold's causal driver is real rates plus currency trust.
trading.invalidator: Oil shock pushes inflation expectations and nominal/real yields higher together.

watchlist.thesis: Oil/Hormuz risk is the macro switch for the whole portfolio.
watchlist.trigger: WTI sustains above $100 or Brent rises materially above $100 with Treasury yields rising.
watchlist.whyNow: CNBC reported oil whipsawing on Iran-US deal developments; this transmits directly to inflation, Fed path and duration multiples.
watchlist.invalidator: Credible de-escalation and oil back below the low-$90s.

watchlist.thesis: Copper/uranium remain structural scarcity assets but need fresh catalysts for aggressive adds.
watchlist.trigger: Fresh verified news on grid/data-center power contracts, mine disruptions, or utility uranium contracting within current reporting window.
watchlist.whyNow: The scarcity thesis is intact, but today's verified 24h news flow is stronger in oil/rates/AI than in copper/uranium.
watchlist.invalidator: AI power demand revised lower or supply response accelerates.

riskWatch.name: Oil-driven inflation shock
riskWatch.description: Sustained WTI/Brent strength would lift inflation expectations, delay Fed easing, raise real yields and compress long-duration AI/REIT multiples. Gold may also suffer if real yields rise.
riskWatch.mitigation: Keep cash buffer; avoid adding duration solely on geopolitical headlines; watch 10Y real yield and oil together.

riskWatch.name: AI concentration/crowding
riskWatch.description: Semis and hyperscalers are carrying much of the index narrative. Strong fundamentals can coexist with sharp valuation drawdowns if yields rise or capex expectations wobble.
riskWatch.mitigation: Stage entries; diversify across foundry/equipment/memory/software infrastructure; avoid single-day chase buys.

riskWatch.name: Singapore rate and credit mix
riskWatch.description: Lower rates pressure bank NIM while high real yields cap REIT rerating. A growth slowdown would add credit-cost risk to banks and occupancy risk to REITs.
riskWatch.mitigation: Hold banks for income; add REITs only when rates and operating data both confirm.

riskWatch.name: Jobs report volatility
riskWatch.description: Payroll/wage surprise can reprice Fed path quickly. Strong data may be bad for duration via yields; weak data may help yields but raise earnings-cycle concerns.
riskWatch.mitigation: Wait for yield reaction before interpreting equity move.

keyEvents.text: US April jobs report: Yahoo Finance reported markets are bracing for the closely watched release.
Iran-US deal / Strait of Hormuz updates: CNBC reported oil and equities are reacting to negotiations and Iran's response.
keyEvents.source: CNBC live market updates, updated May 8

keyEvents.text: Monitor US Treasury nominal and real yields after the jobs/oil reaction; May 7 levels: 10Y nominal 4.41%, 10Y real 1.96%.
keyEvents.source: US Treasury daily yield and real-yield curves

keyEvents.text: Singapore banks read-through after UOB: watch DBS/OCBC price action and NIM/fee-income commentary.

bottomLine.whatMatters: The key regime variable is not the headline conflict itself; it is oil -> inflation -> Fed path -> real yields. That chain determines whether AI multiples, REITs and gold get support or pressure.
bottomLine.whatToDo: Stay invested in the scarcity thesis, keep cash optionality, and deploy only on defined pullbacks with real-yield confirmation. Hold SG banks as income anchors; be slower on REIT adds until yields and operating data align.
bottomLine.whatToIgnore: Ignore first-order narratives like 'geopolitical risk means buy gold' or 'AI demand means chase semis at any price'. Both need the second-order rate/valuation chain checked first.
